screenshotsCaptured.note: Firefox and Webkit timed out at 375px due to extremely long page height (~46000px). Chromium 375px captured successfully.

compositionGate.notes: Overall dark mode composition matches Figma. Layout structure, card grid, filter row, heading + description, and pseudo-element background are all correct. Light mode is a new addition without a Figma reference, evaluated for consistency and readability.

details: Dark charcoal pseudo-element background renders correctly with rounded corners
White heading text (Anton font) is legible and matches Figma proportions
Description text is off-white/light gray and readable
Filter dropdowns have white text and white arrow SVG - correct
SHOW / PER PAGE text and select are visible in white
Resource cards: charcoal background with off-white border, accent-colored tag pills - matches Figma
Policy cards: charcoal background with off-white border, accent tag pills - matches Figma
Blog cards: transparent background, white text, image thumbnails visible - matches Figma
Team member cards: accent (neon-green) background with charcoal text - correct
Testimonial cards: accent background with charcoal text - correct
Card grid: 3-column layout on desktop, correct gap spacing
Load More button visible and styled
No dark pseudo-element background - section sits on the page's off-white/cream background
Heading text is charcoal/black - readable against off-white
Description text is charcoal/dark gray - readable
Resource cards: white background with subtle charcoal/10 border - clean and distinct from page bg
Policy cards: white background with subtle charcoal/10 border - consistent with resource cards
Blog cards: transparent, text is dark charcoal - readable on off-white page
Filter dropdowns have charcoal text and charcoal arrow SVG - correct for light mode
Tag pills remain accent-lighter bg with accent-light border - still readable in both modes
VIEW RESOURCE btn-tertiary text is charcoal in non-hover state - correct
Card corners are rounded-3xl consistently in both modes

darkModeGroupHover.observation: When hovering a resource or policy card in dark mode (card bg transitions from charcoal toward accent), the btn-tertiary 'VIEW RESOURCE' text and arrow become very low-contrast. The text appears to turn a faded cream/off-white color against the transitioning dark card background. This is observed when hovering both the card generally (group:hover) and the btn-tertiary directly (btn-tertiary:hover within the hovered card).
darkModeGroupHover.evidence: Screenshots: archive-hover-resource-dark (group hover) and archive-hover-btn-dark-detail (direct btn hover) both show first card with faded VIEW RESOURCE text. The text contrast against the partially-transitioned card background is very poor.
darkModeGroupHover.rootCause: The dark:hover:text-off-white rule from button.css (line 63) on .btn-tertiary appears to still be winning despite the Archive.css !important overrides. The Tailwind-generated dark:hover:text-off-white may use a CSS layer that has higher priority than the Archive.css rules, or the specificity chain may not be matching correctly in the actual compiled output.

lightModeGroupHover.observation: When hovering a resource or policy card in light mode, the btn-tertiary text stays charcoal. On card hover, background transitions to accent and text remains dark/readable. No white-on-white or contrast issues observed.

lightModeBtnDirectHover.observation: When directly hovering the btn-tertiary in light mode, text stays charcoal. The Archive.css :not(.dark) overrides work correctly.

findings.element: btn-tertiary (VIEW RESOURCE) on resource/policy cards - dark mode hover
findings.figmaValue: Charcoal text when card is in accent hover state
findings.implementationValue: Text fades to off-white/cream on card hover, very low contrast against transitioning card background
findings.recommendation: The dark:hover:text-off-white from button.css is overriding the Archive.css !important rules. The fix should either: (1) Add a more specific override that also covers the btn-tertiary without group:hover being needed (e.g., .archive.dark .card-resource .btn-tertiary:hover, .archive.dark .card-policy .btn-tertiary:hover { color: var(--color-charcoal) !important; }), or (2) Neutralize the dark:hover effect entirely on btn-tertiary within archive card context by adding standalone hover overrides that don't depend on .group:hover.

change.to:
/* Dark mode: override btn-tertiary component hover to prevent contrast issues */
/* Cover both group:hover AND standalone btn-tertiary:hover states */
.archive.dark .card-resource .btn-tertiary,
.archive.dark .card-resource .btn-tertiary:hover,
.archive.dark .card-resource .btn-tertiary:focus,
.archive.dark .card-resource .group:hover .btn-tertiary,
.archive.dark .card-resource .group:hover .btn-tertiary:hover,
.archive.dark .card-resource .group:hover .btn-tertiary:focus,
.archive.dark .card-resource .group:focus-within .btn-tertiary,
.archive.dark .card-policy .btn-tertiary,
.archive.dark .card-policy .btn-tertiary:hover,
.archive.dark .card-policy .btn-tertiary:focus,
.archive.dark .card-policy .group:hover .btn-tertiary,
.archive.dark .card-policy .group:hover .btn-tertiary:hover,
.archive.dark .card-policy .group:hover .btn-tertiary:focus,
.archive.dark .card-policy .group:focus-within .btn-tertiary {
	color: var(--color-charcoal) !important;
}

findings.verification: After fix, hover any resource or policy card in dark mode. The VIEW RESOURCE text and arrow should remain charcoal-colored (dark) at all times - both on group hover and direct btn-tertiary hover.

passedChecks: Dark mode overall composition matches Figma (no regression)
Dark mode charcoal pseudo-element background with rounded corners
Dark mode heading typography (Anton font, white, large display size)
Dark mode description text (off-white, body size)
Dark mode filter dropdowns (white text, white arrow SVG)
Dark mode resource cards (charcoal bg, off-white border, accent tag pills)
Dark mode policy cards (charcoal bg, off-white border, accent tag pills)
Dark mode blog cards (transparent bg, white text, image scale on hover)
Dark mode team member cards (accent bg, charcoal text)
Dark mode testimonial cards (accent bg, charcoal text)
Dark mode blog vertical dividers (white 20% opacity, xl breakpoint)
Dark mode Load More button (white filled for blog)
Light mode: no pseudo-element background, section on page off-white
Light mode heading/description (charcoal text, readable)
Light mode resource cards (white bg, charcoal/10 border)
Light mode policy cards (white bg, charcoal/10 border)
Light mode blog cards (transparent bg, dark text, readable)
Light mode filter dropdowns (charcoal text, charcoal arrow SVG)
Light mode blog vertical dividers (charcoal 20% opacity, xl breakpoint)
Light mode btn-tertiary base state (charcoal text on white cards)
Light mode btn-tertiary hover state (charcoal text maintained)
Light mode card group hover (accent bg, charcoal text maintained)
Card grid spacing consistent between modes
Card border-radius (rounded-3xl) consistent between modes
Tag pill styling works in both modes
Card transition animations work in both modes
data-dark-mode attribute present on section element
Three light mode variations added to test page (blog, resources, policies)

crossBrowserComparison.chromiumVsFirefox: At 768, 1024, and 1440px, Firefox renders identically to Chromium. No browser-specific visual issues observed.
crossBrowserComparison.chromiumVsWebkit: At 768, 1024, and 1440px, Webkit renders identically to Chromium. No browser-specific visual issues observed.
crossBrowserComparison.missingWidths: Firefox and Webkit 375px screenshots could not be captured due to timeout on this very long test page (~46000px at mobile width). Chromium 375px was captured and shows correct mobile rendering.
